descriptions: Dalen Network is affiliated with Farsta yngre.
edges.evidence_quote: the Dalen Network, which appears to be affiliated with Farsta yngre among other things

Kelly Park Compton Crips.description: The Kelly Park Compton Crips are a set of the Crips gang alliance originating in Kelly Park, Compton, California. Known for its connection with rap group N.W.A, members include Eazy-E and MC Ren. The gang has been involved in violent conflicts with rival Piru sets, including the Lueders Park Piru. A police substation established in 1986 reduced criminal incidents in the area.

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Elm Street Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: South Side Compton Crips , Kelly Park Compton Crips , Santana Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips is a set within the Crips alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kelly Park Compton Crips ( KPCC ), formerly known as the Kelly Park Hustler Crips , are a " set " of the Crips gang alliance

descriptions: Elm Street Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Elm Street Piru , Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: The two gangs were in a gang war, with retaliatory killings in 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: Gang war with Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: Mob Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Elm Street Piru , Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips retaliated against Lueders Park Piru for a killing.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kelly Park Compton Crips held the Lueders Park Piru responsible for the death of Stevens, and in retaliation, killed 30-year-old Don Turner

Lenox Street Boys.description: One of the oldest and most dangerous street gangs in Boston, Massachusetts, originating in the Lenox Street Projects in Roxbury possibly dating back to the 1950s. The gang has been active since at least the 1980s, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. They have engaged in long-standing feuds with rival gangs such as the Columbia Point Dawgs. Law enforcement raids in 2011 and 2016 resulted in numerous arrests.

descriptions: Listed as allies in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Orchard Park Trailblazers , Heath Street Gang, Academy Homes Gang, Villa Victoria Park Gang , Everton Geneva Grizzleys

descriptions: Listed as rivals in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Columbia Point Dawgs (long-standing), Vamp Hill Boys, H Block, X-men, Franklin Hill Giants, Greenwood Street Posse, Ruggles St , 1850 Washington St, Castle Square Cubs

descriptions: The two gangs engaged in a violent war over turf in the early 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: According to BPD a bloody war started when a crew of the Columbia Point Dawgs tried to set-up shop in their turf in Ramsey Park where Lenox members frequently congregated.

edges.evidence_quote: The Yüksekova Gang was an illegal organization ... with apparent links to the Turkish Gendarmerie 's JITEM unit

Real Calton Tongs.description: A violent Scottish teenage gang active in the Calton area of Glasgow during the 1960s. The gang financed itself through a protection racket and marked its territory with the slogan 'Tongs Ya Bass'. The area they controlled was nicknamed Tongland.

descriptions: Symbionese Liberation Army is listed as a historical ally of BGF in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Historical: , Black Liberation Army , Symbionese Liberation Army , Weather Underground

descriptions: North Hollywood Boyz 13 is a main rival of Westside Locos 13.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang's main rivals include the nearby Toonerville Rifa 13 , Elmwood st 13, Avenues 13, Vanowen st Locos 13, Mara SalvaTrucha, North Hollywood Boyz 13

Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Ur.description: Colombian paramilitary group founded by the Castaño brothers in northwestern Colombia to retaliate against FARC-EP. It operated mainly in Antioquia and Córdoba departments, protecting members' farms and drug interests while eliminating left-wing sympathizers. It later became the core of the United Self-Defenders of Colombia (AUC).

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU merged to form AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Al conformarse las AUC el 18 de abril de 1997, las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá, las del Magdalena Medio y las de los Llanos Orientales se unieron creando las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU orchestrated the merger that formed AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997 the AUC was formed through a merger, orchestrated by the ACCU, of local right-wing militias.

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: The ACCU orchestrated the merger that formed the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 1997 the AUC was formed through a merger, orchestrated by the ACCU, of local right-wing militias.

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Bloque Metro was linked to and part of ACCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Estaba vinculado a las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (ACCU) y posteriormente a las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Bloque Metro was linked to ACCU as part of its structure.
edges.evidence_quote: Estaba vinculado a las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (ACCU) y posteriormente a las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: The Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá were allies of the Medellín Cartel from 1984 to 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (1984-1992)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: The founders of Clan del Golfo joined ACCU.
edges.evidence_quote: Dairo Antonio y Juan de Dios decidieron vincularse a las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá (ACCU)

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Los Tangueros became ACCU in 1994.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1994, Carlos took control of Los Tangueros, which officially changed their name to the Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Uraba (ACCU).

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU led to the formation of AUC and became its most powerful component.
edges.evidence_quote: After Fidel Castaño's disappearance, his brother Carlos took over the ACCU and eventually led it to the formation of the United Self-Defenders of Colombia (AUC), which grouped other small paramilitary armies organized throughout Colombia.

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU networked with MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: The ACCU began networking with other paramilitary groups such as the MAS

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: Demobilized EPL members integrated into ACCU.
edges.evidence_quote: la integración de desmovilizados del EPL en las ACCU

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU was formed to fight FARC-EP in retaliation for killing their father.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded by Fidel Castaño , Carlos Castaño and Vicente Castaño to retaliate against the assassination of their father Jesús Castaño by FARC-EP guerrillas.

edges.source: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: ACCU fought against FARC-EP guerrillas.
edges.evidence_quote: The ACCU began working with regional military forces ... to crush the guerrillas

edges.target: Peasant Self-Defenders of Córdoba and Urabá
descriptions: EPL considered ACCU as enemies until 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ACCU ( Hasta 2006 )

'Ndrina Cracolici.description: The 'Ndrina Cracolici is a Calabrian 'ndrina based in Maierato, Maida, and Filogaso in the Vibo Valentia province, with branches in Novara and Milan. It is involved in mafia association, murder, extortion, drug trafficking, and illegal arms possession. The group has been in conflict with the Bonavota clan for control of Sant'Onofrio, and was targeted in operations Conquista (2017) and Artemis (2024).

descriptions: Anello and Cracolici had joint commercial interests in Maierato.
edges.evidence_quote: Insieme ai Cracolici e ai Bonavota avrebbero avuto interessi commerciali su Maierato

descriptions: The Cracolici are a rival 'ndrina to the Bonavota, with members killed in 2002 and 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2002 viene ucciso a Maierato Alfredo Cracolici e a Pizzo il 4 maggio 2004 Raffaele Cracolici, appartenenti ad una cosca rivale

descriptions: The Cracolici and Bonavota are in conflict over control of Sant'Onofrio.
edges.evidence_quote: Dall'operazione Conquista del 2017 e conseguente omonimo processo del 2018 ancora in corso si registrano degli scontri con i Bonavota per il dominio di Sant'Onofrio.

descriptions: The Pizzata killed a Nirta-Strangio associate.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 24 gennaio 2013 viene trovato morto ... Vincenzo Femia, referente dei Nirta nella capitale. Autore dell'omicidio la cosca dei Pizzata.

descriptions: Cappello allied with the Lo Piccolo clan from Palermo.
edges.evidence_quote: riuscì a legarsi con la cosca palermitana dei Lo Piccolo.

descriptions: Os Tauras is listed as a rival of Os Manos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivais: CV , V7 , Os Tauras , BNC , Os Aberto , Cebolas , CNG

descriptions: CCA Pará is listed as an ally of B40 in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: GDE , TCP , Nova Okaida, Sindicato do Crime RN, Guardiões do Estado, Bonde do Maluco Bahia, CCA Pará.

descriptions: Al-Nusra loyalists formed Hurras Al-Din to fight HTS.
edges.evidence_quote: Sami Al-Oraydi, alongside other Al-Qaeda loyalists ... mobilised Al-Qaeda personnel in northwestern Syria to establish an anti-HTS front in the region, eventually forming Hurras Al-Din

descriptions: Hurras al-Din was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Hurras al-Din (dissolved in 2025)

descriptions: Members of Thunderbirds MC joined Trust MC, founding the Munich chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1984, some Thunderbirds MC members switched to the Trust MC and thus founded the Trust MC chapter in Munich.

descriptions: The Frisina were allied with the Gallico faction in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Per la prima fazione erano presenti anche i Frisina, i Mazzullo, i, Gramuglia e i Costantino

descriptions: Dirty Dozen clashed with Bad Company MC over territory in 1980.
edges.evidence_quote: The Dirty Dozen came into conflict the following year with the Bad Company MC, a club from New Mexico who were attempting to form a chapter in nearby Globe, resulting in two local Bad Company members, David Howell and Laird Kriley, being badly beaten and ordered to disband.

descriptions: Goryo-kai succeeded the Mio-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Yasuo Takagi formed the Goryo-kai as the successor to the Mio-gumi

descriptions: Mio-gumi is a tertiary organization of the Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: a tertiary organization of the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: The Shimizu family sold its emblem to the head of Mio-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Tanabe sold the Shimizu emblem for circa tens of millions of yen to Yasuo Takagi (head of the Goryo-kai and thereafter the Mio-gumi gang).

descriptions: Ciampà and Corigliano are grouped together with Vrenna and Bonaventura.
edges.evidence_quote: Vrenna-Ciampà-Corigliano-Bonaventura

descriptions: Vrenna, Ciampà, Corigliano, and Bonaventura are grouped together as a single entity.
edges.evidence_quote: Vrenna-Ciampà-Corigliano-Bonaventura
